_meta.document: Revised Master Plan for Bengaluru - 2031 (Draft) — Zoning Regulations
applicable_bylaws: BDA Revised Master Plan 2031 (Draft) — Zoning Regulations

planning_zones.Zone_A: Area falling within the Outer Ring Road (ORR)
planning_zones.Zone_B: Area outside the ORR and up to the conurbation limit
planning_zones.Zone_C: Area beyond the conurbation limit and up to the Local Planning Area boundary — Agricultural zone only

land_use_zones: R    — Residential
C1   — Commercial Cat-1 (petty shops, STD/ATM, tutoring ≤50 sqm, tailoring, bakery)
C2   — Commercial Cat-2 (eateries, gyms, clinics, retail, banks, fuel stations)
C3   — Commercial Cat-3 (corporate offices, malls, hotels, hospitals, cinemas)
C4   — Commercial Cat-4 (warehouses, wholesale, junk yards)
C5   — Commercial Cat-5 (agro mandis, heavy goods markets)
I1   — Industrial: Household Industries (≤5 kW power)
I2   — Industrial: Service/Hi-Tech (IT/BT/BPO, R&D, film studios)
I3   — Industrial: Light Industries
I4   — Industrial: Medium Industries
I5   — Industrial: Heavy Industries (no new I5 in BMA per RMP 2031)
T1   — Transportation Cat-1 (bus bays, metro stations, parking areas)
T2   — Transportation Cat-2 (MLCP, filling stations, LMV workshops)
T3   — Transportation Cat-3 (godowns, truck workshops, bus terminals, telecom)
T4   — Transportation Cat-4 (airports, rail yards, truck terminals, cargo)
PSP1 — Public & Semi-Public Cat-1 (crèches, public libraries, distribution shops)
PSP2 — Public & Semi-Public Cat-2 (police stations, post offices, primary schools)
PSP3 — Public & Semi-Public Cat-3 (hospitals, colleges, govt buildings, fire stations)
PSP4 — Public & Semi-Public Cat-4 (airports and ancillary uses)
PU   — Public Utilities (permitted in all zones: water/sewerage/power/telecom plants)
OS   — Open Spaces / Parks / Playgrounds (up to 2% ancillary FAR 0.50 at G+1)
AG   — Agricultural (Planning Zone C only)
_meta.notes: All values sourced directly from BDA RMP 2031 Draft Zoning Regulations (Tables 1–27) and BBMP Bye-Laws 2003. FAR = Base FAR + Allowable-against-TDR FAR. VERIFY all fields against the official gazette before use in production.

setbacks._source: BDA RMP 2031 Table 1 (front setbacks by road width) and Table 2 (all-round setbacks by height). BBMP Bylaws 2003 Tables 4 & 5 for older buildings.
setbacks._note: Table 1 governs front setback. Table 2 governs all-round setback once building height exceeds 9.5 m. The HIGHER of Table 1 front setback vs Table 2 front setback shall be applied.

front_setback_by_road_width._note: Table 1: Minimum front setbacks for ALL types of buildings w.r.t. road width

all_round_setback_by_height._note: Table 2: Minimum setbacks for all buildings (EXCEPT Industrial) w.r.t. height. Front and Rear/Side values are equal for all rows.
all_round_setback_by_height._note2: Number of floors EXCLUDES basement floors. Stilt floor height COUNTS towards total building height.

rules_and_relaxations.max_building_height_formula: Max height = 1.5 × road width + front setback, subject to a maximum front setback of 16 m
rules_and_relaxations.corner_plots: Both road-facing sides treated as FRONT — apply Table 1 front setback to both sides
rules_and_relaxations.multiple_road_plots: Side facing road from which main motorable access is provided = front; others = rear/side
rules_and_relaxations.irregular_plots: Side/rear setbacks at any critical point shall not be less than 75% of Table 2 value, min 6 m for buildings >15 m. Total setback area must equal full setback requirement
rules_and_relaxations.multiple_buildings_on_site: Distance between buildings = ½ height of tallest building OR 16 m, whichever is LOWER
rules_and_relaxations.stilt_floor_height: Stilt floor height is counted towards total building height for Table 2 calculations. Stilt floor clear height shall not exceed 2.7 m
rules_and_relaxations.residential_plot_upto_120sqm: An open staircase may be permitted in side or rear setbacks
rules_and_relaxations.residential_plot_upto_60sqm: A WC of 1 m × 1.2 m may be permitted in rear setback
rules_and_relaxations.scooter_garage_in_setback: Permitted on side setback ≥1.5 m at rear side, max depth 2.5 m
rules_and_relaxations.mlcp_setback_relaxation: Only 50% of Table 2 setback required for MLCP portion, min 6 m for buildings >G+4 or ≥15 m
rules_and_relaxations.road_widening_relaxation: If proposed road width ≥ 2× existing width: front setback reduced to 50% of Table 2 (non-high-rise) or 25% (high-rise), subject to Table 1 minimum
rules_and_relaxations.setback_not_counted_from: Public open spaces and conservancies cannot be counted as setbacks — must be in owner's plot

items_permitted_in_setback_without_affec: Generator with outdoor acoustic enclosure (exhaust pipe ≥3 m above neighbouring building)
Watchman's cubicle ≤4 sqm at entry/exit — no setback from boundary
Fire control room max 4 m × 4 m — no setback from boundary
Solid waste drying yard / organic waste converter
Transformer/power substation — no setback required (per KPTCL rules)
RCC vehicular ramps ≤6 m wide for movement/parking access
Architectural features not usable for living/storage (not within 5 m height from ground floor if in driveway area)
Cantilever porches, steps and corridor to ground floor entry
Car parking in basement — subject to applicable restrictions
NOTE for non-high-rise: Items pump room, generator, watchman cubicle, sump, STP, solid waste, children's play area, wells — may be abutting building with 1 m setback from boundary

portico.note: No access to top of portico as sit-out

balcony.max_projection_as_fraction_of_setback: 1/3 of setback — whichever is LESS between 1.2/1.75 m and 1/3 of setback
balcony.high_rise_restriction: No projection allowed within 5.5 m vertical clearance from ground level into minimum prescribed setback
balcony.commercial_buildings: Balconies NOT allowed beyond setbacks in commercial buildings

far._note: FAR = Base FAR + Allowable TDR FAR. Total Maximum = sum of both. TDR column blank = TDR not permitted for that bracket.

R._note: Residential use zone. Sites above 20,000 sqm governed by development plan norms (Table 8/9).
R._apartment_min_site: Apartments only on sites >360 sqm (Zone A) or >500 sqm (Zone B); road ≥12.5 m mandatory
R._open_space_requirement: Sites >2000 sqm: min 10% earmarked as open space/play area (min 200 sqm, min 10 m width), handed to RWA. Sites ≥10000 sqm: additional 5% for civic amenities relinquished to Authority

zone_A._source: Table 6: FAR and Ground Coverage for Plots/Sites up to 20,000 sqm — Planning Zone A

plot_120_to_240.max_height_restriction: 12.5 m or Stilt+3/GF+2 if road 9.5–12.5 m

plot_240_to_360.max_height_restriction: 15 m or Stilt+4/GF+3 if road 12.5–15.5 m

zone_B._source: Table 7: FAR and Ground Coverage for Plots/Sites up to 20,000 sqm — Planning Zone B

development_plan_above_20000sqm_non_sdz._source: Table 8: Residential DP on site >20,000 sqm — Non-SDZ areas

development_plan_above_40000sqm_sdz._source: Table 9: Residential DP on site >40,000 sqm — Special Development Zones

C._source: Tables 12 (Zone A) and 13 (Zone B) — Commercial plots up to 12,000 sqm
C._note: For plots >12,000 sqm, non-residential development plan norms (Table 23/24) apply. Plots >2,000 sqm: 10% area earmarked for open spaces.
C._hi_tech_bonus: I-2 (Hi-Tech Industrial): Base FAR = Commercial Base FAR + 0.25; Total Maximum FAR unchanged
C._commercial_in_residential_zone: Ancillary commercial in residential zone: up to 20% or 50 sqm (whichever lower) if road ≥12.5 m
C._petta_area_exception: In Redevelopment Area (Chickpet/Cubbonpet/Cottonpet/parts of Shivajinagar): No setbacks for plots ≤150 sqm except front. For 150–500 sqm, no rear/side setbacks. Max coverage 75%. Buildings ≤100 sqm FAR exempted from parking (parking fee levied instead).

zone_A._source: Table 12: FAR and Ground Coverage for Commercial in Planning Zone A (up to 12,000 sqm)

zone_B._source: Table 13: FAR and Ground Coverage for Commercial in Planning Zone B (up to 12,000 sqm)

permissible_uses_by_road_width._note: * = Subject to space standards in Table 25

I._note: Industrial zones. I-2 (Hi-Tech) uses Commercial FAR + 0.25 Base FAR bonus. I-3 to I-5 use Table 17.
I._no_new_heavy_industry: No further Heavy Industries (I-5), Red Category or quarrying/mining in BMA area
I._open_space_requirement: Plots >2000 sqm: 10% earmarked for open spaces

I2_hitech._note: FAR follows Commercial zone tables (Table 12 or 13 by planning zone). Base FAR = Commercial Base + 0.25. Total Maximum FAR = same as Commercial.

I3_to_I5_other._source: Table 17: Setbacks and Coverage for Industrial Buildings

PSP._source: Table 20: FAR and Ground Coverage in Public and Semi-public Zone
PSP._note: Ancillary uses shall not exceed 20% of site area. For PSP developed as DP/sub-division: non-residential layout norms apply for everything except FAR and coverage (use Table 20).

T._source: Table 22: FAR and Ground Coverage in Transportation and Communication Zone
T._note: For MLCP as independent activity: no FAR or height limitation (subject to fire and airport restrictions). Bus terminals: FAR as per commercial zone; min area 4000 sqm.

above_12000sqm_non_sdz._source: Table 23: Non-residential DP on site >12,000 sqm — Non-SDZ

above_24000sqm_sdz._source: Table 24: Non-residential DP on site >24,000 sqm — Special Development Zones

far_exclusions._note: These areas are NOT counted in FAR computation
items: Service areas: electrical substation/panel room, generator, pump room, AC plant room, solid waste management, fire control room, security/CCTV room
Refuge areas in high-rise buildings (per fire safety norms)
Separate public toilet block in non-residential complex/plot
Architectural features not usable for living or other purposes
Parking areas including driveways and ramps
Swimming pools and attached toilets (common use, constructed in open yard or any floor)
Lift wells and lift machine rooms
Sewerage Treatment Plant (as per KSPCB) and pump rooms
Stilt parking floor — only if fully open on all sides (enclosed/partly enclosed portion counts in FAR)
Basement floors used exclusively for parking and services
Additional car parking in excess of requirements when provided as standalone parking complex or lot
Note: above exclusions apply for plots >4000 sqm only
ground_coverage_exclusions.note: Area for road widening shall be deducted when calculating ground coverage of plot

height_regulations.height_formula: Max height = 1.5 × (road width + front setback), with front setback capped at 16 m
height_exclusions: Service structures above terrace: AC chiller plants, solar panels, radio antennas, lift machine rooms, overhead tanks, staircase headroom cover slabs — capped at 3.5 m above terrace floor
Rain-cover over internal open space — not exceeding 2 m
height_regulations.airport_zone_restriction: Buildings within airport funnel zones must comply with Airport Authority of India height limits. NOC from AAI required.

noc_requirements.noc_for_airport_authority: Wherever applicable (vicinity of Kempegowda Airport and others)
noc_requirements.noc_for_pollution_control: All development plans, apartment buildings and residential layouts under KSPCB category

building_regulations._source: BDA RMP 2031 Section 4.9 and BBMP Bye-Laws 2003 Sections 18–21

lifts._source: BDA RMP 2031 Section 4.9.1(iv) and 4.9.6; BBMP Bye-Laws Section 20.7
lifts.mandatory_above_floors: G+3 (i.e., 4 floors and above) — lift is mandatory
lifts.high_rise_service_lift: All high-rise buildings must provide at least one service lift
lifts.combined_passenger_service_lift: Buildings with <24 units OR <2400 sqm BUA (whichever lower): service lift may double as passenger lift

disabled_access_lift.standard: BIS 13-person passenger lift (Schedule XI, BBMP Bye-Laws)

staircase.high_rise_staircase_enclosure: Buildings ≥15 m height: staircase must be fully enclosed
staircase.buildings_gf_plus_3_and_below: Residential buildings up to G+3: staircase need not be self-contained unit adjacent to external wall
staircase.open_staircase_in_setback: Permitted for residential plots ≤120 sqm (in side or rear setback)

basement._source: BDA RMP 2031 Section 4.9.2; BBMP Bye-Laws 2003 Section 18
basement.definition: Storey partly or wholly below average ground level. Height projection above average ground: max 1.2 m (RMP 2031) / 1.0 m (BBMP 2003)
basement.small_plot_restriction: Sites <200 sqm with depth <20 m: car parking NOT permitted in basement
basement.intensely_developed_area_condition: A-Zone: only 1 basement permitted if site ≥500 sqm with min road width 12 m
permitted_uses: Electrical room, pump room, generator, AC plant room
not_permitted_general: Retail or commercial use (except 3-star+ hotels as noted)
basement.entry_exit: Convenient entry and exit shall be provided. Adequate drainage, ventilation, lighting per NBC
basement.fire_protection: Roof separating basement from floor above must be RCC or equivalent with ≥2-hr fire resistance
basement.waterproofing: Walls and floors must be watertight to resist sub-soil hydraulic pressure
basement.municipalisation_warning: Parking area if misused is liable to be municipalized/taken over without compensation

ramp.slope_commences_from: Building line or front setback edge of property

garage.applicable_to: Individual residential buildings (not apartments) up to G+2 floors or 10 m height
garage.location: Rear corner of site — no side or rear setbacks required
garage.upper_floor: One upper floor ≤3 m permitted; no openings towards neighbouring buildings
garage.max_total_length_m: 1/3 of site length or 6 m (whichever is less)
garage.corner_plot: Garage must be at rear corner diagonally opposite to road intersection

mezzanine_floor._source: BBMP Bye-Laws 2003 Section 20.4; BDA RMP 2031 Section 4.9.1(iii)
mezzanine_floor.permitted_between: Any two floors in residential buildings
mezzanine_floor.accessibility: Accessible only from its lower floor

plinth._source: BDA RMP 2031 Section 4.9.1(ii); BBMP Bye-Laws Section 19
plinth.note: In low-lying areas, plinth height shall ensure adequate gradient for sewerage and flood protection

fire_safety._source: BDA RMP 2031 Section 4.11 and 4.19; BBMP Bye-Laws 2003 Section 23
fire_safety.governing_code: NBC 2016 Part IV — Fire and Life Safety
fire_safety.minimum_exits_above_height_note: At least 2 exits serving every floor for buildings ≥15 m; at least one must be internal stairway

staircase_for_fire_egress.no_lift_shaft_wrapping: Staircase shall not be arranged around a lift shaft unless lift is fully enclosed by fire-rated material

requirements_above_9m._source: BBMP Bye-Laws 2003 Section 23.1 cross-referenced with NBC Part IV
items: Fire escape staircase — minimum 1.2 m clear width
Emergency lighting in corridors and staircases
Fire tender access road — min 4.5 m wide, 4.5 m clearance, 9 m turning radius

requirements_above_15m: Fire lift — minimum 1.1 m × 2.1 m car, 630 kg capacity
Hose reel and extinguishers on every floor
requirements_above_24m: Fire command centre at ground level
Two separate fire escape staircases mandatory
Refuge area every 7 floors — minimum 15 sqm (NOT counted in FAR)

entry_exit_requirements.low_rise_buildings: Two entries of 3.5 m width each, OR one entry of 6 m width
entry_exit_requirements.high_rise_buildings: Two entries of 3.5 m width minimum

requirements._note: 1 car = 1 parking bay of 2.75 m × 5.5 m. Additional parking for part area when part area exceeds 50% of prescribed limits.
requirements.theaters_auditoriums: 1 car per 25 seats (min 20 cars) OR 1 car per 150 sqm — whichever is higher
requirements.multiplex_with_shopping: 1 car per 40 sqm + parking for multiplex per Cinematographic Act
requirements.retail_shops_malls: 1 car per 50 sqm + 1 lorry per 1000 sqm
requirements.wholesale_warehouse: 1 car per 150 sqm + 1 lorry per 500 sqm
requirements.hotel_lodge_tourist_home: 1 car per 4 rooms OR 1 car per 50 sqm (lower applies) + 10% visitors
requirements.star_hotel: 1 car per 2 rooms + 1 car per 50 sqm (excl. room area) for visitors
requirements.industrial: 1 car per 100 sqm + 1 lorry per 1000 sqm
requirements.nursing_home: 1 car per 50 sqm + 10% for ambulances/hospital vehicles
requirements.hospital: 1 car per 75 sqm + 10% for ambulances/hospital vehicles

residential_multi_dwelling.du_above_120sqm: 1 car per DU + 1 additional per 120 sqm above 120 sqm

requirements.residential_single_dwelling: 1 car per 100 sqm BUA (mandatory for plots ≥90 sqm)
requirements.educational: 1 car per 150 sqm + 1 bus per 120 students + drop-off lane (class/faculty/admin rooms only)
requirements.banquet_kalyana_mantapa: 20% of total land area + 1 car per 50 sqm of FAR area (open area: 25 sqm per car)
requirements.it_bt_major_complexes_above_20000sqm: 1 car per 50 sqm + 1 bus per 1500 sqm
requirements.parks_gardens_upto_5ha: 5% of plot area exclusively for parking
requirements.parks_gardens_above_5ha: 10% of plot area exclusively for parking
requirements.stadium: 10% of plot area OR 1 car per 20 seats — whichever is higher

parking.auto_rickshaw_parking: 1 auto space (2.25 m × 1.75 m) per 2000 sqm or part thereof for commercial complexes/multiplexes ≥2000 sqm BUA; must be abutting road; treated as public parking
parking_exemptions: Buildings ≤100 sqm total floor area: no parking insistence in intensively built-up area
Redevelopment/Petta area buildings ≤100 sqm: parking fee levied instead

parking_in_setback.non_high_rise: Car parking allowed in setback; minimum 3.5 m driveway clearance from building
parking_in_setback.high_rise: Car parking allowed in setback; minimum 6 m from building (fire engine movement)
parking_in_setback.commercial_front_setback: May be used for visitors parking only if no compound wall; not counted towards parking requirements

parking.lorry_exemption: Lorry parking not required for household industries (I-1) and service industries

permissible_uses_residential_zone._source: Table 5: Permissible Uses/Activities in Residential Land Use in Planning Zone A and B

ancillary_use_limit.note: No commercial activity if road <12.5 m
ancillary_use_limit.zone_B_main_use: Ancillary use may be permitted as main use in Zone B if site >1000 sqm with 20 m frontage on road ≥18 m

space_standards._source: Table 25: Space Standards for Different Uses (applicable across all zones)

primary_middle_school_1_to_8.note: Min 10% plot area as play area

tdr_and_tod.tdr_governed_by: Section 14-B of KTCP Act; Government Notification UDD 283 BEMRUPRA 2015 dated 04-Mar-2017

conditions: Setback, parking, height regulations must be satisfied for the road width
Only applicable after metro station is operational and confirmed by BMRCL
Charges as per rates notified by Government
metro_tod_zone.calculation: Total FAR = (FAR for plot area within 150 m, capped at 4) + (max allowable FAR for plot area outside 150 m)

conditions: For areas identified as Redevelopment Areas in RMP 2031
Redevelopment area upto 5 Ha: min access road 15 m
Redevelopment area above 5 Ha: min access road 18 m

means_of_access.access_width_3_5m: FAR/height: up to G+1 or 7 m
means_of_access.transformer_reservation: Site >500 sqm: an area of min 3 m × 5 m abutting access road reserved for electrical transformer
means_of_access.service_road_width_aggregation: For plots with service roads: aggregate width of service + main road used for determining FAR and height

rainwater_harvesting._source: BDA RMP 2031 Section 4.14; BBMP Bye-Laws 2003 Section 32
rainwater_harvesting.percolation_pit_fill_material: Small pebbles or brick jali or river sand, covered with perforated concrete slabs
additional_optional_systems: Terrace water collection to sump via filtering tank
Open ground: replace topsoil with river sand for percolation

mandatory_for: Group Housing Schemes / Group Development Schemes with ≥50 units
solar_requirements.requirement: Solar Water Heating System + Solar Lighting System in common areas and outdoor lighting
solar_requirements.occupancy_certificate: Issued only after installation of solar systems to satisfaction of Authority/local authority

tree_planting.site_180_to_240sqm: Minimum 1 tree — in rear setback area
tree_planting.site_above_240sqm: Minimum 2 trees — in rear setback area
tree_planting.apartment_group_housing_dp: 1 tree per 240 sqm of FAR area (or part thereof); trees must be ≥2 m height before OC
tree_planting.sub_division: 40 trees per hectare of land sub-division in addition to site-level plantation

electrical_line_clearances.note: Buildings >10 m height and tall trees not permitted within corridor. Vertical clearance measured from maximum sag of electrical line.

accessibility._source: BBMP Building Bye-Laws 2003 Schedule XI (Bye-law 31.0); BDA RMP 2031 Section 4.9.1(vii)
accessibility.mandatory_trigger: Public and semi-public buildings with covered area ≥300 sqm
accessibility.all_buildings_mandate: All buildings shall be designed for differently abled persons with dedicated parking and supporting facilities

toilet.note: 1 special WC per set with wash basin

signage.note: Contrasting colour, illuminated; no protruding signs

corner_plot.note: Boundary wall to be rounded or chamfered

exceptions: Women's hostels — may exceed standard height with special permission

habitable_room_requirements.lighting_ventilation_opening: Min 1/6 of floor area for residential; 1/5 for offices/educational/hospital (openings excluding doors). Increase by 25% for kitchens

eco_sensitive_buffers.note: Buffers per NGT Order. No development zone in buffer unless for lake-associated activities

noc_requirements_summary._source: BDA RMP 2031 Section 4.19 and BBMP Bye-Laws 2003 Section 3.2(10)
noc_requirements_summary.pollution_control_kspcb: Development plans, apartment buildings and residential layouts under KSPCB category (includes environmental clearance note)
noc_requirements_summary.bda: Change of land use, bifurcation/amalgamation of plot, group housing schemes >4000 sqm

security_deposit._source: BDA RMP 2031 Section 4.20; BBMP Bye-Laws 2003 Section 4.1.5
applicable_to: Residential group housing/multi-dwelling/apartments with more than 4 dwelling units
security_deposit.refund_period: Within 3 years or 30 days from completion certificate — whichever is later
security_deposit.forfeiture: If construction not as per approved plan; separate action under KTCP Act initiated
